title: Session-Reconciler Core: the 2,200-line/28-param forward pass, its edge-triggered disease, and the dormant S19 pure-core destination already in the tree
summary:
## What this subsystem is (HEAD 7efe9935f, worktree `worktree-reconciler`)

Anchor note: the assigned worktree `.claude/worktrees/reconciler` is at **7efe9935f** (the brief's HEAD); the repo root `/data/projects/gascity` is a *divergent* branch (`refactor/sdo-wi1-nudges` @ e0eec587f) that has S30/S31/S33 but **not** S19. All citations below are the **worktree** (7efe). S19 stage 1 (c6b851ac1) IS merged here.

The core is one function, `reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand` (`cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go:1231-3431`, ~2,200 lines), reached through a **5-overload wrapper chain** that each re-thread the args positionally: `reconcileSessionBeads` (1089) → `…AtPath` (1128) → `…AtPathWithNamedDemand` (1161) → `…Traced` (1197) → `…TracedWithNamedDemand` (1231). The innermost signature is **28 parameters** (ctx, cityPath, sessions, desiredState, configuredNames, cfg, sp, sessStore, dops, assignedWorkBeads, rigStores, readyWaitSet, dt, gate, poolDesired, namedSessionDemand, storeQueryPartial, workSet, cityName, it, clk, rec, startupTimeout, driftDrainTimeout, stdout, stderr, trace, …startOptions). They cluster into: desired-world (desiredState/configuredNames/cfg/poolDesired/namedSessionDemand/workSet/readyWaitSet), durable store (sessStore/rigStores/assignedWorkBeads/storeQueryPartial), runtime (sp), mutable cross-tick trackers (dt drainTracker, it idleTracker, gate providerHealthGate), infra (ctx/cityPath/cityName/clk/rec/stdout/stderr), and side-channels (dops, trace, 9 `startExecutionOption` knobs).

**Tick phases (all inside the one function):** Phase 0 heal expired timers + retire duplicate configured-named beads (`:1320`,`:1337`); Phase 0.5 circuit-breaker restore + progress-signature feed (`:1358-1405`); build the coherent typed `infoByID` snapshot from topo-ordered beads (`:1421-1434`); **Phase 1 forward pass** — the bulk, ~1,600 lines (`:1435-3038`) — per-session drain-ack / unknown-state / orphan-or-suspend / rate-limit / failed-create-close / heal / config-drift / drift-relaunch / rollback / zombie-capture, accumulating `wakeTargets`; then `ComputeAwakeSet` (`:3075`) + sleep-policy resolve; the wake/sleep decision loop that fills `startCandidates` and begins drains (`:3121-3366`); `executePlannedStartsTraced` start execution (`:3397`, def `session_lifecycle_parallel.go:2375`); Phase 2 `advanceSessionDrains…` (`:3413`); `return plannedWakes` (`:3429`).

**Arrival-path stamping.** Launch config is now derived once through `buildPreparedStart` (`session_lifecycle_parallel.go:820/828`, S36) — but firstStart/forceFresh/fork classification is still inline (`:926-…`), and identity/priming were historically stamped by whichever path ran. `executePlannedStartsTraced` adds dependency-wave ordering, wake-fairness, per-tick budget, and parallel prep.

**The destination already exists but is DORMANT.** S19 stage 1 landed the pure cores with **zero production callers** (verified: no non-test caller of `deriveConvergeActions`): `session_level_converge.go` (`durableFacts`/`runtimeFacts`/`deriveConvergeActions`/`attemptEligible`/`deriveFirstStart` — pure observe→diff→act, total, idempotent), `session_identity.go` (`desiredSessionIdentity`), `prompt_delivery.go` (`promptDelivery`). Plus the pure `ProjectLifecycle` (`internal/session/lifecycle_projection.go:447`) already backs `healStatePatchWithRollback`. The two-phase priming vocabulary (`primed_at` confirmation + `priming_attempted_at` write-ahead intent + `attemptEligible` backoff) already makes the lost/duplicated-startup-prompt class unrepresentable by construction.

**The ESSENTIAL decision** the loop makes, per session: given (desired config, durable bead facts, observed runtime liveness) choose ONE convergence action from a small set — {wake/start, drain, close, rollback-to-absent, heal-state, prime, restart-in-place, defer}. Everything else is accidental machinery: the 28-param threading + wrapper chain, the hand-maintained `infoByID` coherence protocol, the 32 raw/Info sibling predicates, the unknown-state skip-forever, the inline circuit-breaker/rate-limit/churn/drift/progress-stall accounting interleaved into a 1,600-line `continue`-ladder, and the 5,124-LOC WAL-grade trace subsystem (`session_reconciler_trace_*.go`) that exists *because* the code is otherwise undebuggable.

Scale: reconciler cluster ~20,490 non-test LOC; ~1,941 raw `.Metadata[` sites across 99 session files (257 in the 4 core files); 18 "byte-identical/equivalence-proven" comments in `session_reconciler.go` alone.

painPoints.name: 28-param god function reached through a 5-overload wrapper chain
painPoints.rootCause: Five stacked overloads (session_reconciler.go:1089/1128/1161/1197/1231) re-thread ~24-28 positional args; new inputs append rather than restructure, so callers pass nil/false sentinels (control-dispatcher passes assignedWorkBeads=nil, workSet=nil, storeQueryPartial=false).

painPoints.name: infoByID snapshot coherence maintained BY HAND
painPoints.rootCause: A typed Info snapshot and the raw *beads.Bead are kept in lockstep manually: every Phase-1 mutation must fold its own patch via ApplyPatch/MarkClosed/applyTo (e.g. :1516,:1679,:1729,:2023) under load-bearing hand-proven 'byte-identical' comments; a missed fold silently corrupts the cross-session min-floor scan.
painPoints.errorClass: two sources of truth reconciled by hand

painPoints.name: Edge-triggered per-arrival-path side-effect stamping
painPoints.rootCause: Identity, priming, and firstStart are stamped inside whichever arrival path (fresh spawn / adoption / resume / drift-relaunch / rollback) executed, so a newly added path silently ships a missing side effect; bugs live in the gaps between path-specific stampings.
painPoints.errorClass: invariant enforced per-path instead of by construction

painPoints.name: Stringly-typed state with unknown-state skip-forever
painPoints.rootCause: session state is a raw metadata string; isKnownStateInfo=false leads to `continue` (session_reconciler.go:1523-1532) with no TTL, no escalation, re-logged every tick forever — a typo or version-skew state parks a session indefinitely.
painPoints.errorClass: open-world string enum, no exhaustive handling

painPoints.name: Swallowed probe error authorizes a destructive close
painPoints.rootCause: workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig error collapses to providerAlive=false (session_reconciler.go:1540); !providerAlive then authorizes closeSessionBead on failed-create/orphan arms, so a transient tmux/probe blip can close a live-runtime session.
painPoints.errorClass: error collapsed into a falsy 'dead' signal on a destructive path

painPoints.name: 32 raw/Info sibling predicates, equivalence 'proven' by review
painPoints.rootCause: Each raw-bead predicate has an `…Info` twin (isNamedSessionBead/isNamedSessionInfo, sessionMetadataState/…Info, pendingCreate* pairs) asserted equivalent in a comment, not by construction; updating one and not the other drifts them apart.
painPoints.errorClass: N drifting implementations of one concept

painPoints.name: Cross-cutting accounting inlined into the forward-pass spine
painPoints.rootCause: Circuit breaker, rate-limit stability, churn, drain-ack, config-drift deferral, progress-stall recycle, and idle-probe accounting are all interleaved as inline branches with early `continue`s inside the 1,600-line Phase-1 loop rather than separated observers.
painPoints.errorClass: cross-cutting concerns inlined into one control-flow spine

keep: The pure S19 cores already in the tree and table-testable: deriveConvergeActions + durableFacts/runtimeFacts (cmd/gc/session_level_converge.go), desiredSessionIdentity (session_identity.go), promptDelivery (prompt_delivery.go) — the destination is written, just unwired

The two-phase priming vocabulary: primed_at (durable confirmation) + priming_attempted_at (write-ahead intent) + attemptEligible backoff — already makes lost/duplicated startup-prompt delivery unrepresentable (session_level_converge.go:38-246)

runtimeFacts.observed gating (C5): no live-only or destructive action may be emitted when the runtime was not probed — the correct generalization of storeQueryPartial and the anti-coordinator-kill guard
The pure ProjectLifecycle state projection (internal/session/lifecycle_projection.go:447) already backing healStatePatchWithRollback — a real single state authority exists
The typed session front door (sessionpkg.Store + InfoFromPersistedBead, internal/session/info_store.go) as the single read/write seam confining bead serialization

The convergence reconciler's typed-state switch + (action,error)-wrapped-at-one-site + ReconcileReport idioms (internal/convergence/reconcile.go) as the proven pattern to COPY (S35: copy idioms, do not merge)

Level-triggered convergence with redundant idempotent observers as the reliability model; topo-order dependency-wave scheduling + wake-fairness + per-tick budget in executePlannedStarts (real domain needs)
The per-decision trace taxonomy (RecordDecision site/reason/outcome) as an observability contract — but re-homed onto explicit action objects
kill: The 5-overload wrapper chain (session_reconciler.go:1089-1259) — replace with one Deps struct (stores, provider, clock, recorder, trackers, io) + one Inputs struct (desired-world snapshot)

The hand-maintained infoByID fold protocol (ApplyPatch/MarkClosed after every mutation, 18 'byte-identical' comments) — a plan/materialize split makes stale-snapshot corruption structurally impossible
The 32 raw/Info sibling predicate pairs — one typed Info, one predicate each; delete the raw-bead twins
The unknown-state skip-forever (session_reconciler.go:1523-1532) — exhaustive typed switch with an explicit quarantine/escalation TTL for unrecognized states
The 1,600-line Phase-1 continue-ladder — deriveConvergeActions(durable,runtime) per session + a small ordered action materializer
The WAL-grade trace subsystem AS A CRUTCH (5,124 non-test LOC, session_reconciler_trace_*.go) — a pure, total, table-testable core shrinks this to an action-log projection
The 9 startExecutionOption variadic knobs and the storeQueryPartial/deferSessionClosesOnBoot boolean guards scattered as call-site flags — fold into Deps and into runtimeFacts.observed confidence
The swallowed `err -> providerAlive=false` mapping on destructive paths (session_reconciler.go:1540) — make observation a tri-state (alive/dead/unknown) so 'unknown' can never authorize a close
redesignLevers: deriveConvergeActions is the migration seam: it is pure/total/idempotent and has ZERO production callers today — the path to the destination is 'grow the action vocabulary + wire the act loop' (S19 Stage 3), an incremental strangler, not a rewrite. It supersedes ad-hoc reconcile sites rather than competing with them.

Generalize the primed_at/priming_attempted_at 'durable intent + confirmation' marker pair into a reusable primitive for EVERY at-most-once side effect (close, rollback, drift-relaunch), killing the crash-between-decide-and-act class by construction across the whole loop.

Make runtimeFacts.observed carry probe-error as 'unknown' and gate all destructive actions on it — the 2026-06-09 coordinator-kill class (transient probe blip closes a live session) becomes unrepresentable; storeQueryPartial and deferSessionClosesOnBoot collapse into it.

Wire desiredSessionIdentity into buildPreparedStart AND the adoption barrier so every arrival path stamps the SAME derived identity set — 'identity key omitted because you arrived via the wrong path' becomes impossible.

Compute the inline accounting concerns (circuit breaker, rate-limit, churn, drift, progress-stall) as independent observers that populate runtimeFacts fields, leaving the diff core a pure exhaustive switch — separates observation from decision from materialization (Observe -> Plan -> Materialize).

Finish making ProjectLifecycle the single state authority: healStatePatchWithRollback is already a thin wrapper over it — delete the residual ad-hoc heal string logic and route all state transitions through the pure projection.

Copy convergence/reconcile.go's shape wholesale (typed-state switch, one (action,error) wrap site, ReconcileReport for observability) into the session-reconciler rewrite — S35 explicitly blesses copying the idioms while keeping the domains separate.

Replace the 28-param positional signature + wrapper chain with a Deps struct + a Snapshot(Inputs) value; the reconciler becomes `for each session: plan := derive(observe(session)); materialize(plan, deps)`, one pipeline replacing the ~dozen ad-hoc divergence-reconciliation sites.
filePointers: cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go:1538-1540 (swallowed probe error -> providerAlive=false -> authorizes close)
internal/session/info_store.go:23-149 (InfoFromPersistedBead: ~90-field Info with dual typed+Metadata mirrors)
internal/convergence/reconcile.go:44-84 (typed-state switch blueprint to copy, per S35)
cmd/gc/city_runtime.go:2300,2986 and cmd/gc/cmd_start.go:961 (the 3 live callers, each assembling the 28 args differently)
cmd/gc/session_reconciler_trace_collector.go + trace_types.go/trace_store.go (5,124 non-test LOC trace subsystem — the debuggability symptom)

title: DURABLE-vs-RUNTIME divergence: ~14 ad-hoc comparison sites across 3 engines, 4 runtime-fact types, one nearly-complete unused pure projection
summary:
## What this subsystem is
The "durable" world is the session bead ledger (session-labeled beads carrying ~33 raw metadata keys: state, session_name, pending_create_claim, started_config_hash, last_woke_at, instance_token, generation…). The "runtime" world is live tmux/subprocess/proc-table sessions. Divergence reconciliation = deciding, per session, what to start/heal/drain/close/rollback/relaunch so the two agree.

## HEAD discrepancy (important)
Worktree HEAD is `e0eec587f` on `refactor/sdo-wi1-nudges`, NOT the brief's `7efe9935f`. Neither `7efe9935f` nor S19-stage-1 (`c6b851ac1`, which added `cmd/gc/session_level_converge.go`) is an ancestor of this HEAD — this is a parallel refactor branch. So `session_level_converge.go` does NOT exist here (it lives on `simplify-land/s19-stage2`). What DOES exist here: the typed `ProjectLifecycle` projection and the `Decide*` timer cores. `session_reconciler.go` is 4,971 LOC; the mega-function `reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand` spans `session_reconciler.go:1231-3420` (~2,188 lines, 28 params).

## The observation surface is fragmented (no single RuntimeFacts snapshot)
FOUR runtime-fact struct types, each Running/Alive-bearing, kept coherent by hand: `runtime.Liveness{Running,Alive}` (liveness.go:7); `session.RuntimeObservation{+Attached,LastActive,SessionName}` (manager.go:368); `worker.LiveObservation{+Suspended,RuntimeSessionID,SessionID}` (observe.go:13); `session.RuntimeFacts{Observed,Alive,Attached,Pending}` — the ProjectLifecycle input (lifecycle_projection.go:143). FIVE observation mechanisms: `runtime.ObserveLiveness` (IsRunning+ProcessAlive), `worker.ObserveHandle`/`ObserveRuntimeForInfo` (manager.go:1709, richer — but the reconciler calls narrow accessors `workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig`/`…Alive…`/`…Attached…` each re-probing and discarding all but one field), `ProcessTableScanner.FindRuntimesBySessionID` (/proc scan), `sp.ListRunning` (13 sites), and `sp.GetMeta` env-var identity probe (runningSessionMatchesPendingCreate, session_lifecycle_parallel.go:2257).

## The comparison sites — THREE separate engines + satellites
ENGINE A (mega-function): (1) orphan/`!desired` branch obtains `providerAlive` and swallows probe error to `false` (session_reconciler.go:1538-1540) then decides preserve/rollback/drain/close; (2) desired-branch `observeRuntimeProviderLiveness`→zombie capture (:1967); (3) pending-create identity match→rollback if live runtime is another session (:2025); (4) pending-create lease-expired rollback (:2043); (5) drain-ack live-work cancel via `sessionAttachedForConfigDrift` (:2115+); (6) heal #1 `healStateWithRollback(providerAlive)` (:1692); (7) heal #2 `healStateWithRollback(alive)` — SAME heal, a SECOND runtime observation on the other branch (:2402); (8) config-drift relaunch/drain/restart-in-place ladder (:2540-2850, relaunch :2623); (9) checkStability/checkChurn (:2443/:2455); (10) DecideMaxSessionAge (:2869); (11) DecideIdleTimeout (:2954); (12) wake-if-should-be-awake (:3144).
ENGINE B: adoption barrier (adoption_barrier.go:54) — `sp.ListRunning`+`workerSessionTargetAliveWithConfig` (:156) CREATE a bead for observed runtime, stamping only a SUBSET of identity metadata (config/live hashes left empty for syncSessionBeads).
ENGINE C: process-table orphan sweep (session_beads.go:2227) — reaps runtimes whose bead is closed/absent (runtime→durable, opposite direction).
Satellites: pool-death detection via in-memory prev-vs-current `ListRunning` diff (city_runtime.go:960, edge-triggered, lost on restart); `sweepUndesiredPoolSessionBeads` (city_runtime.go:2685) — durable-only desired-set close that DUPLICATES orphan-close with different guards; `stopRuntimeBeforeSessionBeadMutation` `sp.IsRunning` kill (session_beads.go:~2330); provider-swap (city_runtime.go:1899) and shutdown (:3454). `computePoolDesiredStates` (pool_desired_state.go:108) is correctly PURE over durable state — the good shape.

## The lever already in-repo
`ProjectLifecycle` (lifecycle_projection.go:447) is a pure, total projection emitting DesiredState, RuntimeProjection, Identity, Blockers, WakeCauses, ReconciledState — but ONLY ReconciledState/BaseState/ResetContinuation are consumed (by healStatePatch, session_reconcile.go:1165). Every orphan/drift/pending-create decision re-derives from raw predicates instead. `DecideMaxSessionAge/IdleTimeout` (lifecycle_timers.go) already model the destination (facts→pure ladder→typed decision→caller executes, gather-on-demand) but cover only 2 of ~12 decisions. `convergence/reconcile.go` is the crash-safe typed-state-switch blueprint.

painPoints.name: Runtime observed N times per session per tick through N narrow single-field accessors
painPoints.rootCause: No per-session RuntimeFacts snapshot; heal#1 uses providerAlive (worker probe), heal#2 uses alive (ObserveLiveness), attach/pending/identity each re-probe. Same session, multiple probes, at different tick moments → TOCTOU disagreement.
painPoints.errorClass: observation fragmentation / TOCTOU on live state

painPoints.name: Swallowed runtime-probe error authorizes destructive orphan close
painPoints.rootCause: workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig error is mapped providerAlive=false (session_reconciler.go:1540); a transient tmux/list blip then routes a LIVE session to the not-running close-bead arm. Only storeQueryPartial and assigned-work re-checks partially guard it.

painPoints.name: Four runtime-fact types + 20 raw/Info predicate twins kept equal by review, not construction
painPoints.rootCause: Dual bead vs session.Info representation and 4 Running/Alive structs each hand-synced; 25+ 'equivalence-proven'/'byte-identical' comments in session_reconciler.go assert equality that the type system does not enforce.

painPoints.name: One concept, N drifting implementations of orphan/undesired close
painPoints.rootCause: A session bead absent from desired state is closed by the mega-function !desired arm AND by sweepUndesiredPoolSessionBeads AND by ≥4 closeSessionBeadIf* helpers, each with its own protection-window/assigned-work/runtime-stopped guards that can disagree.
painPoints.errorClass: duplicated reconciliation logic with divergent guards

painPoints.name: Rich pure projection computed then thrown away
painPoints.rootCause: ProjectLifecycle already derives DesiredState/RuntimeProjection/Identity/Blockers/WakeCauses, but the reconciler consumes only ReconciledState; orphan/drift/pending-create decisions re-derive the same facts via ad-hoc predicates, so the projection and the imperative paths can diverge.

painPoints.name: Edge-triggered reconciliations that vanish on restart
painPoints.rootCause: Pool-death detection diffs an in-memory prevPoolRunning against a fresh ListRunning (city_runtime.go:960); adoption stamps only a metadata subset (config/live hashes empty). Deaths/identity-fields between the crash and the next observation are lost — the #3872 dropped-edge class.
painPoints.errorClass: edge-triggered state held off the durable ledger

painPoints.name: Both reconciliation directions live in unrelated code with different probes
painPoints.rootCause: durable→runtime (start/close in mega-func via ObserveLiveness) and runtime→durable (adopt via ListRunning; reap via proc-table FindRuntimesBySessionID) are three engines with three observation mechanisms and no shared diff, so cross-direction races (adopt vs reap vs close) are only prevented by scattered ad-hoc guards.

keep: ProjectLifecycle (internal/session/lifecycle_projection.go:447): pure, total, no runtime I/O, typed LifecycleView with DesiredState/RuntimeProjection/Identity/Blockers/WakeCauses — this IS the diff core, just under-consumed

DecideMaxSessionAge / DecideIdleTimeout (internal/session/lifecycle_timers.go): the exact target shape — facts→pure ladder→typed TimerDecision→caller executes side effects, with gather-on-demand actions (TimerActionGatherPending) that keep I/O cost/order with the caller
internal/convergence/reconcile.go: the crash-safe blueprint — typed-state switch, (action,error) wrapped at ONE site, per-item errors captured not fatal, startup reconcile of interrupted beads
computePoolDesiredStates (cmd/gc/pool_desired_state.go:108): desired state as a PURE function of durable beads+config with zero runtime observation — the correct desired-side shape to preserve
session.RuntimeFacts (lifecycle_projection.go:143) as the canonical runtime-input type feeding the projection — the right seam; it just needs to be the ONLY runtime-fact type

The WAL-grade trace subsystem's decision vocabulary (TraceSite* / TraceReason* / TraceOutcome*): a ready-made enumeration of every decision a converge core must emit, and a built-in conformance oracle for a rewrite
kill: The 2,188-line 28-param mega-function reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand (session_reconciler.go:1231-3420) — interleaves observation, decision, and mutation for ~12 concerns in one scope

Per-question narrow runtime accessors (workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig/…Alive…/…Attached…/…LastActivity…) that each re-run worker.ObserveHandle and discard all but one field — collapse to one observe-once
The four parallel Running/Alive struct types (runtime.Liveness, session.RuntimeObservation, worker.LiveObservation, session.RuntimeFacts) — one runtime-facts type
The 20 raw/Info predicate twins and 25+ 'equivalence-proven'/'byte-identical' review-asserted comments in session_reconciler.go — a single typed input eliminates the pairs and the proofs
Two separate healStateWithRollback calls per session on different branches with two different runtime observations (session_reconciler.go:1692 vs :2402)
sweepUndesiredPoolSessionBeads (city_runtime.go:2685) as a second orphan-close implementation — fold into the one converge core's undesired→close action
In-memory-only edge state: prevPoolRunning pool-death diff (city_runtime.go:960) — re-derive from durable+observed each tick, level-triggered
error→providerAlive=false collapse (session_reconciler.go:1540) — a probe error must be a distinct RuntimeProjectionUnknown fact, never license to close
redesignLevers: Introduce ONE per-session RuntimeFacts observed exactly once per tick (extend session.RuntimeFacts to carry Running/Alive/Attached/LastActive/Pending/IdentityMatch/Unknown), built by a single worker.ObserveHandle call; every decision reads it — kills the TOCTOU and the 4-type zoo

Make ProjectLifecycle the single diff core: extend LifecycleView (or add deriveConvergeActions(durable, RuntimeFacts, desired) []Action) so it emits the FULL ordered idempotent action list (start/heal/drain/close/rollback/relaunch/reap/adopt), then a thin executor applies actions under existing locks — replacing ~14 ad-hoc sites

Model 'runtime observation failed' as a first-class RuntimeProjectionUnknown (it already exists as an enum value, lifecycle_projection.go) so no error can ever collapse to false-and-close; Unknown → defer with TTL, never destroy

Unify BOTH directions: adoption (runtime-present/bead-absent → create) and proc-table reap (runtime-present/bead-closed → kill) become two actions the SAME converge core emits from one observed set — deleting Engine B and Engine C as separate loops and their cross-engine race guards

Generalize the DecideMaxSessionAge/IdleTimeout gather-on-demand pattern to ALL decisions: the pure ladder returns GatherX actions; the caller supplies facts and re-decides. This bounds runtime I/O and keeps the whole decision in one testable pure function

Make identity/priming stamping a level-triggered action (StampIdentity if any of the 13 canonical fields absent) so fresh-spawn, adoption, resume, drift-relaunch, rollback all converge to the SAME complete metadata set — closing the edge-triggered stamping-subset gaps where most of the 100+ bugs live

Reuse the TraceSite/TraceReason/TraceOutcome vocabulary as the Action enum and as a golden-parity oracle: run old imperative paths and new deriveConvergeActions side-by-side, asserting identical trace-decision streams, to migrate behavior-preserving behind a pin

Idempotence as the acceptance test: re-running deriveConvergeActions after actions land must yield the empty list — a single property test that makes whole classes of oscillation (the creating↔asleep ping-pong the code fights with ga-mf1 comments) unrepresentable

title: The sound blueprint (internal/convergence) + the dispatch control-bead engine: what makes convergence crash-safe, where dispatch drifts, and whether S35's "don't unify" verdict holds
summary:
**HEAD note:** worktree is at `e0eec587f` (+2 nudge-refactor commits past the brief's `7efe9935f`, both unrelated to this subsystem), so convergence/dispatch are effectively at the brief's HEAD. `session_reconciler.go` is now **4971 LOC** (brief said 5101). `internal/convergence` is 4058 LOC non-test.

**What makes convergence sound (confirmed against code).** The six S35 idioms are real and current: (1) **typed states in durable metadata** — named constants `StateCreating/Active/WaitingManual/WaitingTrigger/Terminated` (metadata.go:50-56), dispatched by a total switch with an explicit `default: unknown state` error (reconcile.go:92-124); (2) **idempotency keys per unit of work** — `IdempotencyKey(beadID,iter)` + `FindByIdempotencyKey` (handler.go:19, reconcile.go:131); (3) **speculative-pour-before-commit + persisted-outcome replay** — next wisp poured at step 3b *before* gate eval (handler.go:244-275), `skipGateEval` replays persisted outcome (handler.go:280-298); (4) **dedup-marker-LAST ordering** — `last_processed_wisp` is the monotonic marker, dedup skips `iter<=lastProcessed` (handler.go:199); (5) **startup reconciler safe from any interrupted point** (reconcile.go `Reconciler`); (6) **single-writer-per-bead** documented at handler.go:143-145.

**Correction #1 — S35 gap #1 is now structurally closed *in the steady-state path* (already merged).** S30/#4042 (79daedbd6) added `commit(writes, preMarker, marker)` (handler.go:773-807): the marker is a dedicated trailing parameter, making "marker written last" **unrepresentable to violate at a call site.** This upgraded the invariant from comment-enforced to type-enforced — the exact fix the S35 note demanded. **But** it is only wired in Handler (handler.go) + manual.go (lines 83,378); the **Reconciler still writes the marker by hand** via bare `SetMetadata`/`CloseBead` ordering (reconcile.go:497→503→510, completeTerminalTransition), so the startup path retains the very drift S35 warned about. This is a residual *intra*-convergence gap.

**Where dispatch drifts from the blueprint (concrete).** Dispatch is *more* convergence-like than the brief implies: it has typed states (`DrainStatePending/Expanding/Expanded/Completing/Succeeded/Failed`, values.go; `SpawnStateSpawning/Spawned`), a total switch (drain.go:48-58), a resumable durable **manifest** with per-row progress (`unit-created`/`root-created`/`wired`, drain.go), idempotency keys **and epoch-fencing CAS** via `molecule.Attach{IdempotencyKey, ExpectedEpoch}`→`ErrEpochConflict` (molecule.go:250-266) — a wired concurrency guard **convergence lacks.** The real drifts: (a) **outcome multiplexed onto the error channel** — `ErrControlPending` ("retry later, not a failure") and `ErrControlGraphMalformed` ("quarantine me") returned from ~40 sites, disambiguated by `errors.Is` at the serve loop (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:251-260); convergence models "nothing to do" as a **typed `ActionNoAction` value**, not an error. (b) **`ControlResult.Action` is a free string** with ~20 literals ("pass","retry","hard-fail","drain-expanded","fanout-spawn","noop"…) — no exhaustiveness — vs convergence's typed `ReconcileAction`/`HandlerAction` constants. (c) **lineage reconstructed by 4 string-surgery heuristics** in `latestAttemptFromCandidates` (control.go:1394-1472) instead of a durable pointer — precisely S38's target.

**Correction #2 — dispatch has NO separate startup reconciler, and that's a strength.** `ProcessControl` is a single re-entrant function re-entered every tick, branching on durable typed state and resuming from the manifest checkpoint. This is *closer* to the ideal K8s single-`Reconcile(obj)` shape than convergence's **dual Handler+Reconciler split**, where the Reconciler partially *re-implements* Handler transitions (reconcile.go paths 1a/1b/2/3/4 duplicate iterate/terminate) and must agree by hand across ~5 writer paths (Create, Handler, manual×3, trigger, Reconciler).

**S35 "don't unify" verdict holds** — convergence imports zero session/worker/runtime; session_reconciler.go has zero convergence refs; disjoint domains. But the note's blanket "copy the idioms, don't import" **conflates two things**: the *state machines* (must stay separate) vs the *mechanical helpers* — `commit(writes,preMarker,marker)`, idempotency-key mint/find, epoch-fence CAS, per-item batch error isolation — which are domain-agnostic (operate on `store+id+key`) and *should* be shared. The redesign blueprint is not "convergence" — it is **dispatch's single re-entrant Reconcile shape + convergence's structural guarantees (commit-helper, typed action) + dispatch's epoch-fence CAS**, extracted as small shared primitives.

painPoints.name: Reconciler startup path bypasses the commit() marker-ordering helper
painPoints.rootCause: reconcile.go's completeTerminalTransition/reconcileMissingState write state+close+last_processed_wisp via bare SetMetadata (reconcile.go:497-510), not commit(); marker-last is comment-enforced only on the crash-recovery path — the exact gap S35 flagged, closed for Handler (S30 #4042) but not here.

painPoints.name: Dispatch multiplexes retry/quarantine signals onto the error return
painPoints.rootCause: ErrControlPending (not-done) and ErrControlGraphMalformed (quarantine) travel the error channel from ~40 sites, disambiguated by errors.Is at the serve loop; a missed/incorrect errors.Is turns a benign retry into a fatal or an unwanted quarantine.

painPoints.name: ControlResult.Action is a stringly-typed outcome
painPoints.rootCause: runtime.go:23 declares Action as a bare string; ~20 literal values are scattered across control/drain/fanout/ralph with no typed enum and no exhaustiveness check, unlike convergence's typed ReconcileAction/HandlerAction constants.

painPoints.name: Convergence single-writer is a documented assumption, not a wired lock
painPoints.rootCause: handler.go:143-145 depends on single-writer-per-bead for race-free snapshot reads but has NO CAS/epoch guard; dispatch's molecule.Attach already wires exactly this (ExpectedEpoch→ErrEpochConflict, molecule.go:260-266). Concurrent controllers on one root can stale-read.

painPoints.name: findLatestAttempt reconstructs lineage from 4 string-surgery heuristics
painPoints.rootCause: latestAttemptFromCandidates matches attempt roots by prefix/ref surgery (control.go:1423-1459) plus kind-skip rules instead of reading a durable gc.control_for bead-ID pointer; ref-shape changes silently break attempt recovery and can fatal the serve loop.

painPoints.name: Convergence dual Handler+Reconciler duplicate the same transitions
painPoints.rootCause: the startup Reconciler (reconcile.go paths 1a/1b/2/3/4) re-derives state transitions the steady-state Handler already implements (iterate/terminate/waiting), so two code paths across ~5 writer entry points must be kept in agreement by hand; dispatch avoids this with one re-entrant ProcessControl.

keep: commit(writes, preMarker, marker) with the marker as a dedicated trailing parameter — makes 'dedup marker written LAST' unrepresentable to violate at a call site (handler.go:773-807, S30/#4042). The single most important idiom to carry forward.
Typed-state total switch with an explicit default:unknown-state error, enabling resume-from-any-interrupted-point (reconcile.go:92-124; drain.go:48-58)

Idempotency key per unit of work + FindByIdempotencyKey: every pour/attach is lookup-able and re-entrant, so a mid-op crash recovers by key rather than duplicating (handler.go:19; reconcile.go:131; molecule.go:250)
Speculative-pour-before-commit + persisted-outcome replay: converts at-most-once priming into exactly-once across a crash between eval and commit (handler.go:244-298)
Epoch-fencing CAS in molecule.Attach (ExpectedEpoch→ErrEpochConflict) — the WIRED concurrency guard convergence only assumes; keep and generalize it (molecule.go:260-266)

Dispatch's single re-entrant ProcessControl with a durable manifest checkpoint and NO separate startup reconciler — the level-triggered resume shape closest to a K8s Reconcile(obj) (runtime.go:108-150; drain.go)
Per-item error isolation in the batch reconcile: one bead's failure is captured in the report and the scan continues (reconcile.go:60-68)
Graceful-degradation reads on the recovery path (missing last-processed wisp treated as iteration 0 rather than blocking, handler.go:189-198)
kill: The Reconciler's hand-rolled marker ordering — route reconcile.go's terminal/creation writes through the same commit() helper so marker-last is structural on the crash path too (reconcile.go:496-511)

ErrControlPending / ErrControlGraphMalformed as sentinel control-flow on the error channel — replace with a typed total outcome (Done/Pending/Quarantine/Fail) returned in the RESULT value (runtime.go:96-100)
ControlResult.Action free string — replace with a typed enum mirroring ReconcileAction (runtime.go:23)
The 4-heuristic string-surgery lineage matcher in latestAttemptFromCandidates — replace with the S38 durable gc.control_for bead-ID pointer (control.go:1394-1472)

Convergence's startup Reconciler duplicating Handler transition logic — collapse into one re-entrant reconcile that branches on typed state and calls the SAME transition functions (reconcile.go paths 1a/1b/2/3/4 vs handler.go iterate/terminate)

The unspoken assumption that copying idioms means duplicating the mechanical helpers per-subsystem — the marker-commit helper, idempotency-key format, and epoch-fence are domain-agnostic and should be shared, not re-typed
redesignLevers: SYNTHESIZE, don't copy one subsystem: the destination is dispatch's SINGLE re-entrant Reconcile(obj) shape (level-triggered, resume-from-durable-typed-state, no separate startup path) + convergence's STRUCTURAL guarantees (commit-helper marker-last, typed action enum, speculative-pour replay) + dispatch's epoch-fence CAS. Neither subsystem alone is the blueprint.

Extract shared MECHANICAL micro-primitives (this refines S35's blanket 'copy don't import'): (a) markerOrderedCommit(store,id,writes,preMarker,marker); (b) idempotency-key mint+find; (c) epochFence CAS; (d) per-item batch error isolation. All operate on store+id+key, are domain-agnostic, pass the primitive test, and become MORE useful as more reconcilers adopt them. The STATE MACHINES (which states exist, what transitions mean) stay per-domain — that is the real content of S35's separation.

Make the reconcile outcome a TOTAL typed sum type (Done | Pending | Quarantine | Fail{err}) returned as the result, so 'retry later' and 'quarantine' can never be confused with a real error and exhaustiveness is compiler-checked — closing the ErrControlPending/Action-string drift in one move.

Adopt the S38 durable gc.control_for bead-ID pointer to delete the last string-surgery lineage reconstruction in dispatch (one ID-equality + integer-max replaces 4 heuristics); the shadow-parity test already sketched in the S38 spec is the migration guardrail.

Wire the single-writer invariant as an epoch/CAS fence for convergence too (it already exists in molecule.Attach) so snapshot-read safety stops being a documented assumption — closing S35 gap #2 that is still open.

Fold convergence's dual Handler+Reconciler into one re-entrant reconcile branching on typed state, eliminating the by-hand agreement obligation across ~5 writer paths and the transition-logic duplication in the startup path.

Treat the manifest-with-per-row-status (drain.go) as the reference pattern for a durable, resumable multi-step checkpoint — generalize it as the standard way any reconcile step records partial progress.
filePointers: internal/convergence/handler.go:143-145 (single-writer documented assumption, no wired lock)
internal/convergence/handler.go:161-390 (HandleWispClosed 9-step: speculative pour 244-275, skipGateEval replay 280-298, dedup 199)
internal/convergence/reconcile.go:84-124 (typed-state total switch with default:unknown-state error)
internal/convergence/reconcile.go:496-511 (completeTerminalTransition: marker ordered BY HAND, bypasses commit() — residual S35 gap on the startup path)
internal/dispatch/runtime.go:108-150 (single re-entrant ProcessControl kind-switch — no separate startup reconciler)
internal/dispatch/drain.go:48-58 (DrainState typed-state total switch — the dispatch analog of reconcile.go:92)
internal/molecule/molecule.go:250-266 (IdempotencyKey dedup + ExpectedEpoch CAS fence → ErrEpochConflict; the wired guard convergence lacks)
engdocs/simplification/specs/S38-control-for-lineage-spec.md (durable gc.control_for lineage pointer — the dispatch drift fix)
git 02d4f8a34:engdocs/contributors/convergence-vs-session-reconciler.md (S35 note — NOT landed in main; decision holds, doc lives only on simplify/audit-2026-07-07)

title: The TYPE + TRACE layer: 87-field hand-coherent Info, a decorative state machine, and a 5-KLOC WAL that exists because there is no pure decision core
summary:
Scope note: my subsystem files are byte-identical between the brief's `7efe9935f` and this worktree HEAD `e0eec587f` (only nudge files differ on branch `refactor/sdo-wi1-nudges`; the two nudge commits are the same typed-front-door pattern applied to the nudge cluster — a useful in-repo template).

**Dual representation, coherent only by hand.** `session.Info` (internal/session/manager.go:74-364) is an 87-field flat struct; ~50 fields are RAW string mirrors of metadata keys, each carrying a multi-line comment justifying why a typed field would "lose fidelity" (consumers compare trimmed vs untrimmed, or parse RFC3339/int) — e.g. `MetadataState` vs `State` are TWO fields for one concept (manager.go:187), and reconciler classifiers key off the raw one. Three codecs re-implement the same per-key mapping: `InfoFromPersistedBead` (info_store.go:23), `ApplyPatch` (72-arm switch, info_apply_patch.go:37) that RE-DERIVES the projection, and `MarkClosed` (info_apply_patch.go:239). Coherence is enforced at runtime, not by construction: `TestInfoApplyPatchMatchesReprojection`, `TestInfoMarkClosedMatchesReprojection`, plus 75 `*Info` sibling predicates in cmd/gc proven equal to their raw-bead twins by `TestSessionClassifierInfoEquivalence` (52 pairs), `TestSessionSnapshotInfoEquivalence`, `TestNamedSessionInfoEquivalence` over finite fixtures.

**Four parallel typed projections of one bead, none authoritative.** Besides `Info`, `ProjectLifecycle`→`LifecycleView` (lifecycle_projection.go:447) is a SEPARATE projection fed by `LifecycleInput`, itself built two ways — `LifecycleInputFromMetadata` (raw map, :200) and `LifecycleInputFromInfo` (from Info, :225) — a fifth codec re-cracking 13 keys; `CircuitStateFromMetadata` (circuit_state.go:62) is a fourth projection deliberately NOT on Info. Plus `transportFromMetadata`, `ProviderFamilyFromMetadata`, `ParseTemplateOverridesFromInfo`.

**Stringly-typed open state space.** `State` is a bare `string` (manager.go:27); `SleepReason` too (sleep_reason.go:15). `State("garbage")`/`SleepReason("garbage")` compile. The reconciler's understanding of state is a hardcoded allowlist `knownSessionStates` (session_reconcile.go:1438); any bead outside it → `isKnownStateInfo` false → `continue` (session_reconciler.go:1523-1532): skip FOREVER, no TTL, no escalation, re-logged + re-traced every tick. Normalization is scattered across `normalizeInfoState`, `canonicalLifecycleState`, `compatStateForBase`, `projectBaseState` (~48 sites). Two near-identical case lists (`IsDeliberateSleepReason` vs `shouldResetContinuation`) are documented as MUST-diverge, kept apart only by comment (lifecycle_projection.go:696, sleep_reason.go:47).

**Two disjoint mutation vocabularies.** The clean reducer `Transition` (state_machine.go:154, `ErrIllegalTransition` 409-mapping) is wired ONLY as an API/Manager validation gate (manager.go:1108/1180/1386) — its own header admits it's "the first step toward a single explicit reducer" that never landed. The actual reconciler write path is 21 ad-hoc `*Patch` constructors (lifecycle_transition.go) writing a union of 42 keys, each `state=X` unconditionally, never consulting the table (28 non-test call sites). The state machine is decorative.

**Swallowed error on a destructive path.** `providerAlive bool` (session_reconciler.go:1538-1540) coerces a probe error to `false`, which then authorizes orphan-close/rollback — `bool` cannot represent "unknown/probe-failed."

**The WAL as a symptom.** The trace subsystem is 5,124 LOC across 8 cmd/gc files: a bespoke append-only log DB with 16 MB segments, CRC32 batch integrity, corrupt-segment quarantine, head-file crash recovery, seq monotonicity, low-space degraded modes, age/byte pruning, per-template auto-"arms," and causality (`ParentRecordID`/`CausedByRecordIDs`). Its 60-field record (trace_types.go:345) tags every decision with ~70 SiteCodes, ~55 ReasonCodes, ~60 OutcomeCodes — a shadow taxonomy of the god function's branches, externalized because the function is unreadable. It exists precisely because there is no pure decision core to inspect; a whole `gc trace` CLI + reconciler-debugging.md runbook depend on it. A pure `deriveActions(observation) -> []Action` collapses the trace to {input facts, output actions} per (session,tick) — deterministically replayable — dissolving the WAL machinery and the taxonomy (Action variant + Reason field replace SiteCode/ReasonCode/OutcomeCode).

painPoints.name: Info: 87 fields, ~50 raw string mirrors kept coherent by hand
painPoints.rootCause: Bead→Info projection is duplicated in InfoFromPersistedBead, a 72-arm ApplyPatch, MarkClosed, and 75 cmd/gc *Info sibling predicates; parity is proven by fixture tests, not construction, so any divergence is a latent bug.

painPoints.name: Stringly-typed open State space → skip-forever
painPoints.rootCause: State/SleepReason are bare string aliases with no closed set; unknown state falls outside knownSessionStates allowlist and the reconciler continues past it every tick with no TTL or escalation.
painPoints.errorClass: illegal state is representable (open string enum)

painPoints.name: Decorative state machine vs 21 ad-hoc patch recipes
painPoints.rootCause: Transition table encodes legal (cmd,from)->to but is only an API validation gate; the reconciler mutates state through 21 independent *Patch constructors that set state unconditionally, so legality and key-sets have no single source of truth.
painPoints.errorClass: no single source of truth for state transitions

painPoints.name: Four+ parallel typed projections of one session bead
painPoints.rootCause: Info, LifecycleView (via LifecycleInput built two ways), CircuitState, and transport/provider/template codecs each re-crack the same metadata map independently instead of one Observe step.

painPoints.name: bool liveness swallows probe errors on destructive path
painPoints.rootCause: workerSessionTargetRunning error is coerced to providerAlive=false, which authorizes orphan-close/rollback; a two-valued type cannot carry alive/dead/unknown.
painPoints.errorClass: two-valued type for a three-valued fact

painPoints.name: 5,124-LOC WAL trace compensating for an unreadable god function
painPoints.rootCause: No pure decision core means causality can't be derived from function I/O, so every branch is instrumented into a bespoke segmented+CRC+quarantine log with a ~185-code SiteCode/ReasonCode/OutcomeCode taxonomy.
painPoints.errorClass: observability machinery substituting for a pure, inspectable core

painPoints.name: Divergent-but-near-identical hand-curated policy lists
painPoints.rootCause: IsDeliberateSleepReason and shouldResetContinuation are near-duplicate switch lists that must intentionally differ by one element each; freshWakeConversationResetKeys is a third manually-synced key list — all kept correct only by comment.
painPoints.errorClass: duplicated policy lists kept apart by comment

keep: ProjectLifecycle (lifecycle_projection.go:447) is the sound in-repo blueprint: a PURE, total projection into a typed observed/desired vocabulary (BaseState/DesiredState/RuntimeProjection/IdentityProjection/LifecycleBlocker/WakeCause). This is the Kubernetes observed-vs-desired split done right and should be the spine of the redesign.
state_machine.go Transition table + ErrIllegalTransition/IllegalTransitionError: a clean reducer skeleton and a good 409-mapping error type — the right shape, just not yet the write path.

The typed-vocabulary direction (SleepReason alias, the lifecycle_projection enums, CircuitState) — turning raw strings into named constants at the read edge is correct; it just needs to become closed/exhaustive rather than open aliases.
CircuitState confinement (circuit_state.go): keeping breaker timers/counters OFF Info is a good boundary decision — not every metadata cluster belongs in the god struct.

The equivalence oracles (TestSessionClassifierInfoEquivalence, *MatchesReprojection) as MIGRATION SCAFFOLDING: they let a rewrite prove byte-for-byte parity against the legacy path before deleting mirrors — reuse them as the legacy-parity pin, then delete once fixes activate.
MetadataPatch as the atomic write unit (lifecycle_transition.go:29) and its marker-clearing semantics — a good primitive to keep under a single reducer.
The already-landed 'close the Nudges class' typed front door (HEAD e0eec587f) — the in-repo template for confining a raw-string cluster behind a typed seam.
kill: Info's ~50 raw-string mirror fields plus their fidelity-justification comments (manager.go:116-364) — replace with a single typed observation projected once at the codec edge.
ApplyPatch's 72-arm re-derivation switch and MarkClosed (info_apply_patch.go) — a pure reducer over typed state removes the need to re-fold a metadata patch onto a snapshot.
The 75 *Info sibling predicates in cmd/gc and the 52-pair TestSessionClassifierInfoEquivalence — with one representation there is nothing to keep equivalent.
The dual feed LifecycleInputFromInfo vs LifecycleInputFromMetadata (lifecycle_projection.go:200/225) and the Info.State vs Info.MetadataState split (manager.go:187) — one projection, one state field.
The 21 ad-hoc *Patch constructors as the primary write path (lifecycle_transition.go) — fold into one transition reducer whose table is the single source for legality AND key mutation.

knownSessionStates allowlist map + the skip-forever unknown-state branch (session_reconcile.go:1438, session_reconciler.go:1523) — a closed State sum type makes the allowlist and the silent skip unrepresentable.

The bespoke 5,124-LOC WAL trace store (segments/CRC/quarantine/arms/head-recovery) and the ~185-code Site/Reason/Outcome taxonomy — reduce to structured logging of {typed observation, []Action} emitted by the pure core.
providerAlive bool (session_reconciler.go:1538) — replace with a tri-state Liveness{Alive,Dead,Unknown}.
redesignLevers: Make State and SleepReason CLOSED types (a Go sum via sealed interface, or an exhaustively-linted iota enum with a mandatory default→error): unknown-state-skip-forever and sleep-reason typos become compile/lint errors, and every switch over state is provably total.

Project each session bead ONCE per tick into a single typed Observation{DurableFacts, RuntimeFacts}; feed a PURE, total deriveActions(Observation) -> []Action ordered idempotent list; a thin reducer applies actions under existing locks. This one move deletes the dual representation, the 75 mirrors, the 3 equivalence oracles, the ApplyPatch re-derivation, and the LifecycleInput dual feed.

Unify the decorative Transition table and the 21 patch constructors into ONE reducer reduce(state, event) -> (state, MetadataPatch): legality guard and key mutation derive from a single table; illegal transitions and stray key writes become unrepresentable.
Introduce tri-state Liveness{Alive,Dead,Unknown}; require Dead (never Unknown) to authorize any orphan-close/rollback — the tmux-blip→destructive-close class becomes unrepresentable.

With a pure core, the trace IS the input Observation plus the output []Action per (session,tick) — deterministically replayable in a table test — so the WAL/segments/CRC/quarantine/arms machinery and the Site/Reason/Outcome taxonomy collapse into an Action variant + Reason field, and gc trace becomes a projection over structured action logs.

Generalize the two existing correct templates — ProjectLifecycle (pure typed projection) and the just-landed Nudges typed front door — into the codec edge for the whole session domain; keep the equivalence oracles temporarily as the legacy-parity pin, then delete them with the mirrors.

Fold the near-identical policy lists (IsDeliberateSleepReason, shouldResetContinuation, freshWakeConversationResetKeys) into data-driven predicates keyed on the closed SleepReason type so their intentional divergence is expressed as explicit per-variant fields, not comments.

title: The Reconciler Outer Shell: supervision, lifecycle, and the serial-tick head-of-line problem (HEAD 7efe9935f)
summary:
## What the outer shell actually is (correcting the brief)

At HEAD the "outer 30s tick + config reload + graceful shutdown + in-memory trackers" the brief attributes to `cmd/gc/controller.go` have **already moved into `CityRuntime` in `cmd/gc/city_runtime.go`** (3494 LOC). `controller.go` (1421 LOC) is now the **socket/lock command surface + config-parse + gracefulStopAll helpers**: `acquireControllerLock` flock on `.gc/controller.lock` (controller.go:107), `startControllerSocket`/`handleControllerConn` command router (stop/stop-force/ping/poke/reload/converge/trace-arm/session-circuit-reset, controller.go:124-237), `controllerSocketPath` with a sha256 `/tmp` fallback past the 100-char UNIX_PATH_MAX (controller.go:93), `watchConfigTargets` fsnotify+200ms debounce (controller.go:727), `tryReloadConfig` parse/validate (controller.go:902), and `gracefulStopAll` two-pass interrupt→kill (controller.go:977). `controllerLoop` (controller.go:1138) is now a thin shim that builds a `CityRuntime` and calls `.run()`. `runController` (controller.go:1234) wires the lock, socket, token, controllerState + its 3 background loops (startBeadEventWatcher/startEmergencyEventRelay/startMaintenanceLoop, api_state.go:377-455), and the API mux.

## Three nested supervision levels

1. **Machine supervisor** (`cmd_supervisor.go`, 2630 LOC): singleton via flock on `supervisor.lock` (cmd_supervisor.go:214) + `supervisor.sock` + an EADDRINUSE `/health` probe to detect a duplicate binder of the shared API port (cmd_supervisor.go:373). `runSupervisor` (1226) owns a `cityRegistry`; `reconcileCities` (1604) is its reconcile loop driven by a ticker + `reconcileCh` (SIGHUP/socket). It spawns one goroutine per city (2239) with `recover()`, `panicRecord` exponential backoff (10s→5min, 2288-2298), and `initFailRecord` for cities that fail `prepareCityForSupervisor`. Shutdown modes `None/PreserveSessions/Destructive` (276-282) with escalation to `supervisorHardExit`.
2. **Per-city `CityRuntime.run()`** (city_runtime.go:364): a **single-goroutine** loop. Startup is a bespoke 6-phase retry ladder (`retryStartupStep`, 445-648: adoption-barrier → convergence-init → config-reload → startup-orders → route-recovery → startup reconcile → convergence-startup). Steady state is `for{select}` over `ticker.C` (patrol), `pokeCh`→`pokeDB.fired` (debounced event wake, `tickDebouncer` 843), `nudgeWakeCh`, `controlDispatcherCh`→`ctrlDB.fired`, `convergenceReqCh`, `ctx.Done` (727-769). **Reload acceptance runs on its own goroutine** (699-711) so a slow tick can't block accept; the reload *work* still runs on the tick goroutine.
3. **In-memory trackers** — the brief lists 4; there are **5** parallel nil-guarded interface+`memoryXxx` singletons, each rebuilt from config on reload: `crashTracker` (crash_tracker.go), `idleTracker` (idle_tracker.go), `wispGC` (wisp_gc.go, interval-gated `lastRun`), `orderDispatcher`/`memoryOrderDispatcher` (order_dispatch.go: `lastRunCache`, `gateBackoffUntil`, `inflightN`), plus `maxSessionAgeTracker` (max_session_age_tracker.go). All "in-memory only — intentionally lost on controller restart… same as Erlang/OTP" (crash_tracker.go:9).

## The disease, at the shell level

`tick()` (city_runtime.go:929-1300) runs **~30 sequential phases** (pool-death `ListRunning`, order dispatch, reaps/sweeps, `loadDemandSnapshot` w/ scale_check shell-outs, syncBeads, `beadReconcileTick`, wispGC, convergence) instrumented only by `recordPhase` timing — **no per-phase context deadline**. Only order-dispatch gates (`gateOpenWorkBounded`, orderGateTimeout=8s, order_dispatch.go:530/624/1934) and order drains (reloadOrderDrainTimeout=1s) are bounded. Since the whole loop is one goroutine, a slow `cr.sp.ListRunning("")` (pool-death, 960) or scale_check subprocess **stalls patrol/poke/convergence/nudge/control-dispatcher together** — this is the brief's "pool check can stall the whole tick / no per-check timeout." `safeTick` (794) recovers **all** panics and continues; a latent nil-deref panics every tick and only surfaces as repeated stderr lines. `reloadActiveTTL=10min` force-clear (city_runtime.go:1596-1625) is a band-aid that exists *because* a wedged tick strands the reload slot.

painPoints.name: Unbounded synchronous work on the serial reconcile goroutine (head-of-line blocking)
painPoints.rootCause: tick() runs ~30 phases inline on one goroutine with no per-phase deadline; a slow ListRunning/scale_check/store-read stalls patrol, poke, convergence, nudge and control-dispatcher together.

painPoints.name: safeTick swallows every panic with no escalation ladder
painPoints.rootCause: safeTick recover()s all panics, logs to stderr, and continues; a persistent invariant bug panics every tick with no counter, circuit-break, or crash-loop backoff (unlike the supervisor's per-city panicRecord).
bugRefs: cmd_supervisor.go:2288 (contrast: panicRecord backoff exists only at goroutine granularity)

painPoints.name: Five parallel nil-guarded tracker singletons rebuilt by hand on reload
painPoints.rootCause: crash/idle/wisp/maxSessionAge/orderDispatcher each re-implement the same build-from-config + nil-check + rebuild-on-reload lifecycle with no shared abstraction; reloadConfigTraced hand-rewires each one.

painPoints.name: Config reload is a 340-line in-place live mutation on the tick goroutine
painPoints.rootCause: reloadConfigTraced mutates provider/dops/cfg/trackers/dispatcher/convergence-scopes/watcher in place; a provider swap calls gracefulStopAll mid-reload, polling up to shutdown_timeout and stalling the loop, instead of build-new-then-atomic-swap.

painPoints.name: Ephemeral-vs-durable state split is ad hoc per tracker
painPoints.rootCause: crash counts reset to 0 on every controller restart (a crash-looper regains full restart budget), idle data is cleared on reload, order lastRun/gateBackoff survive reload but not restart — no single declared policy for what survives a restart vs a reload.

painPoints.name: Startup reconcile is a separate hand-maintained copy of the steady-state tick
painPoints.rootCause: run()'s 6-phase retryStartupStep ladder duplicates the load-snapshot/reap/build/sync/reconcile sequence that tick() also performs; the two paths must be kept equivalent by hand rather than being one level-triggered function run at boot and on tick.
bugRefs: city_runtime.go:2133 (bootReconcile flag threads the divergence into beadReconcileTick)

painPoints.name: Two subtly-different flock+socket singleton implementations
painPoints.rootCause: supervisor (supervisor.lock + supervisor.sock + EADDRINUSE /health probe) and controller (.gc/controller.lock + .gc/controller.sock + sha256 /tmp path fallback + pre-listen os.Remove race) each hand-roll 'one instance per scope' with divergent stale-socket handling.

painPoints.name: Unstructured background goroutines with no supervision tree
painPoints.rootCause: signal handler, socket accept loop, reload-accept, nudge-wake listener, startup watchdog, config-watcher event loop, and controllerState's bead-event/emergency/maintenance loops are all `go func()`ed independently with ad-hoc lifecycle; none has a declared restart strategy the way an OTP child spec would.

keep: The single-writer serial reconcile goroutine: all domain mutation happens on one goroutine so there is no shared-state locking on session/order state — this matches a K8s controller worker and is sound; it only needs per-item bounding so one slow item can't block the rest (city_runtime.go:727-769)
Level-triggered patrol ticker as the correctness backstop with poke/wake as a low-latency edge overlay, coalesced by tickDebouncer so bursts collapse to one fire (city_runtime.go:731-754, 843-903)
flock + Unix control-socket command surface as the singleton + out-of-band control channel (controller.go:107,124)
shutdownOnce + preserve-sessions-for-readoption: sessions deliberately outlive the controller so a restart re-adopts live runtimes (city_runtime.go:3417-3486, cmd_supervisor.go:1172)
Supervisor per-city panicRecord exponential backoff crash-loop + initFailRecord — the RIGHT escalation model, just applied only at goroutine granularity (cmd_supervisor.go:2277-2301)
The reload-accept-on-a-separate-goroutine split so a slow tick never blocks reload acceptance (city_runtime.go:699-711)

order-dispatch's gateOpenWorkBounded per-check timeout + gate backoff — this bounded-probe pattern is exactly what the rest of the tick lacks and should be generalized (order_dispatch.go:530,624,1956)
safeTick's panic ISOLATION (a transient Dolt EOF must not kill the whole city) — keep the isolation, add escalation (city_runtime.go:794)
kill: The 5 parallel nil-guarded tracker interfaces (crash/idle/wisp/maxSessionAge/orderDispatcher) — collapse into one ephemeral-state holder with an explicit persist-vs-lose declaration per field
reloadConfigTraced's 340-line in-place field surgery, including the mid-reload gracefulStopAll provider swap — replace with build-new-immutable-snapshot-then-atomic-swap (city_runtime.go:1709-2053)
The reloadActiveTTL 10-minute force-clear of a stranded reload slot — a pure band-aid for wedged ticks that disappears once ticks are bounded (city_runtime.go:1596-1625)
The bespoke 6-phase startup retry ladder as a distinct code path from tick() — fold into one crash-safe level-triggered reconcile run once at boot then on tick (city_runtime.go:445-648)
Unconditional panic-swallow with no counter/circuit-break in safeTick (city_runtime.go:794)
controllerLoop the compatibility shim once callers move to CityRuntime directly (self-documented as removable, controller.go:1132-1197)
Two independent flock+socket singleton implementations — unify into one
redesignLevers: Bounded per-phase execution: wrap every reconcile phase (and the whole tick) in a context deadline by generalizing order-dispatch's gateOpenWorkBounded into a phase-runner. Directly kills the head-of-line-blocking pain point without touching the single-writer model.

K8s-style keyed workqueue reconciler: replace the one serial mega-tick with a bounded pool of workers pulling keyed items (per-session, per-order, per-convergence-scope) off a rate-limited workqueue with per-item retry/backoff. Head-of-line blocking vanishes, and crashTracker + order gateBackoff + supervisor panicRecord all collapse into ONE workqueue.RateLimiter primitive — an error class made unrepresentable.

Explicit persistent-vs-ephemeral state split: one typed ControllerEphemeralState (debounce timers, in-flight counts — genuinely OK to lose) vs everything durable in beads. Crash counts that MUST survive a controller restart to keep a quarantine enforced move to a bead; make 'lost on restart' a deliberate, tested property, not 5 ad-hoc maps.

Copy-on-reload immutable config snapshot: build a fresh CityRuntime view from the new config, validate fully, then atomically swap a pointer under serviceStateMu. Reload becomes 'publish a new immutable snapshot'; no in-place surgery, no gracefulStopAll on the loop goroutine.
One level-triggered crash-safe reconcile function run at boot and on every tick — delete the startup/steady-state duplication and the bootReconcile branch entirely.

OTP-style supervision spec for the goroutine zoo: declare the tree (supervisor → city → {reconciler, order-dispatch, convergence, nudge, maintenance, bead-event-watcher}) with a per-child restart strategy (one-for-one + backoff), replacing hand-rolled go func()+recover()+panicRecord scattered across cmd_supervisor.go, controller.go, city_runtime.go and safeTick.

Escalation ladder for panics/errors: transient→retry silently, repeated→count+backoff+event, persistent→circuit-break the single item while keeping the city alive. Ties directly into the workqueue rate limiter.

title: Reconciler Bug Corpus & Testability: eight error classes, and why a pure-core design collapses them into table tests
summary:
## Grounding & branch caveat
This worktree (`refactor/sdo-wi1-nudges`, HEAD `e0eec587f`) **diverged BEFORE the brief's HEAD `7efe9935f`**: S19-stage1, S20, S34, S36 are on mainline but NOT here (`git merge-base --is-ancestor 7efe9935f HEAD` = NO). I read the S19 pure cores via `git show 7efe9935f:` and confirmed the rest against local files. Confirmations of the brief: the 28-param god function is real (`cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go:1231`, `reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand`); the stringly-state skip-forever is real (`session_reconciler.go:1520-1533` — raw `fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "...unknown state %q")` + `continue`, **re-logged every tick, no TTL/escalation**); the WAL-grade trace subsystem is ~5,124 LOC across 8 `session_reconciler_trace_*.go` files and exists *because* the fused decision path is otherwise unobservable (`reconciler-debugging.md` even says use it "when the reconciler does something unexpected").

## Bug-number mapping (brief numbers → commits/semantics, verified)
The brief's numbers are GitHub issue refs cited in code/spec comments, not bd IDs. Mapped via `git log --grep`: **#3872** edge-triggered events dropped ⇒ sessions permanently unreconciled (S19/S36 cure); **#3849** durable start-hash present but transcript gone ⇒ `--resume` hard-fails, pane dies every tick (crash-loop, S19 `deriveFirstStart`); **#2073** live runtime under *absent* durable intent = pane leak (S19 `actionRollbackRuntimeToAbsent`; PendingCreateLease trim `6d9579def`); **#2285/#2083** pool new-demand counted durable-only without runtime liveness ⇒ ghost/duplicate spawn (S24); **#2085/#2389** unknown-state skip needs throttling (S20, `a3293d3fe` — **absent on this branch**); **#2987** fail-closed liveness gate on *all* destructive paths (S16 F1/F2/F3, `207178aaf`); **#1053** one `launchWorkflow` chokepoint (S14); **#1029** infoByID fold through one tick front door (S23); **#1938/#3892** typed `RoutedControlBead` predicate shared (S21); **#3279** de-correlate 429 respawns with jitter+THROTTLED (retry storm); **#2403** convergence-scope "restart the controller" taxonomy — **deleted on mainline by S34, still present here** at `convergence_tick.go:72,187,191`.

Code-cited exemplars corroborate the classes: **#2574** reset→drain-ack re-fires a phantom 2nd restart (`session_reconciler.go:2284,2347`); **#127** freshly-created session drained before its startup window (`:2490,2518`); **#3630** named-session spec-absent collapse during boot spuriously drains and loses in-session context (`session_types.go:79,116` `suspendDeferrals`); **#3288** boot-tick pool-sweep must be deferred or it hangs/prematurely closes (`city_runtime.go:2126,2217,2289`).

## The destination is already in-repo (mainline)
`session_level_converge.go@7efe9935f` is the sound shape: `durableFacts`(one bead-key per field, no precedence ladder) × `runtimeFacts`(observed/live/primedEnv) → `deriveConvergeActions()` returning an **ordered, total, idempotent** `[]sessConvergeAction` (rollback dominates; identity-heal; two-phase priming). Sibling pure cores: `prompt_delivery.go` (single priming policy) and `session_identity.go` `desiredSessionIdentity()` (canonical identity contract stated once). These are **pure, table-testable, and currently INERT** (not wired to the act loop until S19 Stage 3). The clean blueprint the redesign should copy (per S35): `internal/convergence/reconcile.go` (typed-state switch, `(action,error)` wrapped at one site, idempotency keys, marker-last ordering, crash-safe startup reconcile).

## Testability, as it actually is
Good news: the god function **is** callable with fakes — `newReconcilerTestEnv` (`session_reconciler_test.go:270`) wires `beads.NewMemStore()` + `runtime.NewFake()` + `clock.Fake` + `events.Discard`, and ~56K LOC of tests exercise it (`session_reconciler_test` 10,273; `build_desired_state_test` 11,732; `session_lifecycle_parallel_test` 6,898; `session_reconcile_test` 2,959; seeded `session_lifecycle_chaos_test` 1,719). The tax: tests assert on **side effects** (a returned `woken int`, then `store.GetMetadata`) not on a plan, because decision is fused with I/O; every test rebuilds the full 28-arg call (`:384`) and adding a field ripples through 5 overload shims (`reconcileSessionBeads/AtPath/AtPathWithNamedDemand/Traced/TracedWithNamedDemand`, `:1089-1231`). Global mutable `sessionCircuitBreakerSingleton` is hand-reset under a lock in the env ctor. The crash-recovery paths are only reachable through the `//go:build integration` boot (`test/integration/gastown_reconciler_test.go`, real `gc` supervisor subprocess, `provider="file"`, 100ms patrol) because the trackers are in-memory. A pure-core design turns each error class into a `(durable×runtime)→[]action` table row, makes idempotence a one-line property, promotes the existing seeded chaos test to a deterministic-simulation tier asserting one liveness invariant, and demotes the 5,124-LOC trace subsystem from load-bearing debugger to optional telemetry.

painPoints.name: Path-dependent edge-triggered stamping
painPoints.rootCause: Fresh-spawn/adoption/resume/drift-relaunch/rollback each stamp their own subset of identity+priming metadata edge-triggered; a new path silently omits a side effect and a dropped edge is never re-applied (no level-triggered re-derivation).

painPoints.name: Durable/runtime divergence reconciled ad-hoc
painPoints.rootCause: ~a dozen sites reconcile persisted intent vs observed runtime independently: live-under-absent pane leak, durable-start-hash vs missing transcript resume-death, pool demand counted durable-only ignoring liveness. No single observe→diff→act with typed facts.

painPoints.name: Stringly-typed state → skip-forever
painPoints.rootCause: Session state is a raw metadata string; an unrecognized value hits `if !isKnownStateInfo(info) { fprintf stderr; continue }` — re-logged every tick with no TTL, no escalation, no dead-letter. Stuck sessions are invisible.

painPoints.name: Swallowed error on destructive path
painPoints.rootCause: A transient store-query-partial or provider blip mapping to not-alive could authorize a rollback/orphan-close of a genuinely live session; only defended reactively via fail-closed liveness gates and storeQueryPartial defers, not by construction.

painPoints.name: Duplicate-spawn from N drifting launch paths
painPoints.rootCause: Multiple launch/pool-demand paths each decide to spawn; pool new-demand double-counted durable beads without runtime liveness, and launch lacked one dedupe chokepoint — producing ghost/duplicate sessions.

painPoints.name: Delivery ordering — at-most-once priming
painPoints.rootCause: Stamping primed_at BEFORE the nudge loses the startup prompt forever on a crash (at-most-once); inverting gives unbounded re-nudge (at-least-once). Correct fix needs a write-ahead attempt marker + explicit confirmation + bounded backoff.

painPoints.name: Dual-representation kept coherent by hand
painPoints.rootCause: Session state exists as both raw beads.Bead metadata and a typed session.Info snapshot, plus raw/Info predicate pairs each 'equivalence-proven' by review not construction; drift between the two forms produces wrong decisions.

painPoints.name: Phantom lifecycle transitions from stale metadata
painPoints.rootCause: Leftover reset/drain-ack/start metadata on the snapshot re-fires spurious restarts or drains: reset→drain-ack phantom 2nd restart, fresh session drained pre-startup-window, named spec-absent boot collapse, boot-tick sweep premature close.

keep: internal/convergence/reconcile.go — the sound blueprint (typed-state switch, (action,error) wrapped at one site, idempotency keys, marker-last write ordering, crash-safe startup reconcile); S35 says COPY its idioms, do not merge domains

S19 pure cores already merged on mainline (deriveConvergeActions/deriveFirstStart/attemptEligible pure+total+idempotent; two-phase write-ahead priming marker in prompt_delivery.go; desiredSessionIdentity in session_identity.go) — the destination shape, keep and widen
The fake-harness triad: beads.NewMemStore() + runtime.NewFake() + clock.Fake + events.Discard — it is fast, hermetic, and already exercises the reconciler in-process
The seeded, deterministic chaos test (session_lifecycle_chaos_test.go, 1,719 LOC over the fake harness, no tmux) — it is the working seed of a deterministic-simulation tier
The legacy-parity pin (sessTranscriptUnknown reproduces the durable-only decision byte-for-byte) as a permanent regression net that lets fixes activate one at a time
Fail-closed-on-destructive is already the established pattern (storeQueryPartial defers, provider-alive defers) — keep the intent, promote it to a type
kill: The 28-param god function reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand + its 5 overload shims (session_reconciler.go:1089-1231) — decision fused with I/O
The stringly-typed raw metadata state and its skip-forever branch (session_reconciler.go:1520-1533) — no TTL, no escalation, re-logged every tick
The ~5,124-LOC session_reconciler_trace_* subsystem AS A LOAD-BEARING DEBUGGER (keep only a thin telemetry slice) — it is a symptom of an unobservable fused decision path
The global mutable sessionCircuitBreakerSingleton hand-reset under a lock in newReconcilerTestEnv — inject it instead
The #2403 'restart the controller' scope taxonomy (convergence_tick.go:72,187,191) — already deleted on mainline by S34; dead weight on this branch
Per-path stamping branches (fresh/adoption/resume/drift/rollback each stamping their own subset of identity/priming) — replaced by one deriveConvergeActions plan
Tests that assert on side effects (woken int + post-hoc store.GetMetadata) reconstructing the full 28-arg call — replace with plan-equality table tests
redesignLevers: deriveConvergeActions already exists and is pure — widen it to own EVERY Phase-1 decision (identity, priming, firstStart, rollback, drain, pool-demand) and return ONE ordered idempotent plan; the reconciler becomes a dumb executor. Every error class then collapses to a (durableFacts×runtimeFacts)→[]action table row.

Make a destructive action UNCONSTRUCTIBLE without a proof: an actionRollback/Close value can only be built from an observed-live-or-known-absent witness, so swallowed-error-authorized-close (#2073/#2987 class) becomes unrepresentable in the type system rather than guarded reactively.
Replace the raw state string with a typed session-state enum whose 'unknown' is an explicit typed state carrying a TTL + escalation, killing skip-forever (#2085/#2389 class) by construction.

Project the in-memory controller trackers (crash/idle/wispGC/order) as beads so crash-recovery converges by redundancy (honors 'work persists') AND becomes testable without a real process restart — the current only reason the integration boot exists.

Promote the seeded chaos test into a first-class deterministic-simulation tier: thousands of tick sequences with injected crashes/partials asserting ONE liveness invariant ('all started work eventually completes'), enabled once decisions are a pure returned plan.
Idempotence as a free property: re-running deriveConvergeActions after its actions land must yield []; assert it once generically instead of per-scenario side-effect archaeology.
Single-home the priming two-phase marker (already in prompt_delivery.go) as the template for every at-most-once vs at-least-once delivery decision in the reconciler.
